body:
## Task
Final go-live checklist.

## Pre-Launch
- [ ] All QA tests pass (#56)
- [ ] Custom domain DNS verified
- [ ] SSL certificate active
- [ ] Email sender domain configured
- [ ] All forms tested in production
- [ ] Admin team trained on Service Hub

## Launch
- [ ] Publish all portal pages
- [ ] Enable membership gating
- [ ] Send announcement to existing customers
- [ ] Monitor for errors in first 24 hours

## Post-Launch
- [ ] Monitor page load performance
- [ ] Check CRM data integrity
- [ ] Verify all workflows triggering correctly
- [ ] Address any customer-reported issues
- [ ] Decommission old Next.js portal (when ready)

## Depends On
- All previous phases complete

milestone.title: Week 5: Forms + Native Services + Deploy
milestone.description: Phase 5 (all forms) + Phase 7 (KB + support) + Phase 8 (deploy all pages, QA)

title: [P8] Go-live: publish all pages + DNS + monitoring

body:
## Task
Complete end-to-end testing of the entire portal before go-live.

## Test Scenarios:

### Authentication
- [ ] New user registration → welcome email → login
- [ ] Existing user login → dashboard
- [ ] Password reset flow
- [ ] Non-member → redirect to login
- [ ] Session persistence

### Dashboard
- [ ] Stats display correctly (order counts, plan info)
- [ ] Recent orders show latest 5
- [ ] Production alert shows if active order
- [ ] Quick actions navigate correctly

### Orders
- [ ] Order list loads with all customer orders
- [ ] Status badges display correctly
- [ ] Order detail page loads via dynamic URL
- [ ] Production timeline renders stages
- [ ] Only shows logged-in user's orders (security check)

### Profile
- [ ] Hair profile data displays if exists
- [ ] Empty state shows for new users (onboarding)
- [ ] Form submission creates/updates profile
- [ ] Photos display correctly

### Customization
- [ ] Templates grid displays saved templates
- [ ] "Create new" form works
- [ ] Empty state for no templates

### Shop
- [ ] Products load from HubDB
- [ ] Category filtering works
- [ ] Sort works

### Billing
- [ ] Current plan displays
- [ ] Plan comparison cards render
- [ ] Plan change form submits

### Support
- [ ] Ticket form creates ticket
- [ ] KB articles accessible
- [ ] Service Hub portal accessible

### Cross-Cutting
- [ ] Mobile responsiveness (all pages)
- [ ] Performance: pages load < 3s
- [ ] All forms submit and create/update records
- [ ] All email notifications trigger correctly
- [ ] Sidebar navigation active states work
- [ ] 404 and 500 error pages display correctly

milestone.title: Week 5: Forms + Native Services + Deploy
milestone.description: Phase 5 (all forms) + Phase 7 (KB + support) + Phase 8 (deploy all pages, QA)

title: [P8] End-to-end testing + QA

body:
## Task
Create actual website pages in HubSpot using the uploaded theme templates.

## Pages to Create (Content > Website Pages):

| Page | URL Path | Template |
|------|----------|----------|
| Dashboard | `/portal/` | portal-dashboard.html |
| Orders | `/portal/orders` | portal-orders.html |
| Order Detail | `/portal/orders/{slug}` | portal-order-detail.html (dynamic) |
| Profile | `/portal/profile` | portal-profile.html |
| Customization | `/portal/customization` | portal-customization.html |
| Shop | `/portal/shop` | portal-shop.html |
| Billing | `/portal/billing` | portal-billing.html |
| Invoices | `/portal/invoices` | portal-invoices.html |
| Locations | `/portal/locations` | portal-locations.html |
| Settings | `/portal/settings` | portal-settings.html |
| Support | `/portal/support` | portal-support.html |

## For Each Page:
1. Select the correct template
2. Set the URL slug
3. Configure modules with any static content
4. Apply "Portal Customers" access group (gating)
5. Preview and test

## Steps
- [ ] Create each page listed above
- [ ] Apply membership gating to all pages
- [ ] Configure module content/settings where needed
- [ ] Preview all pages
- [ ] Verify GraphQL data loads correctly on preview

## Depends On
- #54 theme upload
- #49 access group

body:
## Task
Upload the completed theme to HubSpot Design Manager.

## Steps
- [ ] Run `hs theme validate src/` — fix any warnings/errors
- [ ] Run `hs upload src/ portal-theme` — upload to HubSpot
- [ ] Verify in Design Manager: all templates, modules, queries visible
- [ ] Confirm GraphQL queries resolve correctly
- [ ] Test module previews in Design Manager

## Commands
```bash
cd customer-portal-2.0
hs theme validate src/
hs upload src/ portal-theme
```

## Depends On
- All Phase 2-4 work complete

body:
## Task
Configure automated email notifications for portal events.

## Transactional Emails:
1. **Welcome Email** — on registration
2. **Profile Complete** — hair profile submitted
3. **Order Confirmation** — new order created
4. **Order Status Update** — status changes
5. **Production Milestone** — key production stages
6. **Invoice Ready** — new invoice
7. **Support Ticket Created** — confirmation
8. **Support Ticket Updated** — agent reply
9. **Plan Change Confirmation** — plan change processed

## Steps
- [ ] Create email templates for each notification
- [ ] Create workflows to trigger on CRM events
- [ ] Test all email triggers
- [ ] Configure sender domain and DKIM
- [ ] Link to notification preferences (#48)

## Depends On
- #50 registration workflow
- All Phase 5 forms

body:
## Task
Configure HubSpot Service Hub native customer portal for ticket management.

## Configuration
1. Enable Customer Portal in Service Hub settings
2. Configure on portal subdomain
3. Link to membership access group
4. Style to match theme
5. Set up ticket pipeline: Customer Support
6. Stages: New → In Progress → Waiting on Customer → Resolved → Closed

## Steps
- [ ] Enable Service Hub customer portal
- [ ] Configure ticket pipeline and stages
- [ ] Style portal to match theme
- [ ] Connect to membership access group
- [ ] Test round-trip: submit ticket → agent responds → customer sees response

## Depends On
- #45 support ticket form
- #49 membership access group

body:
## Task
Create the automated workflow for new customer onboarding through membership.

## Workflow: New Customer Registration
**Trigger:** Contact submits membership registration form

**Actions:**
1. Add contact to "Portal Customers" access group
2. Set `onboarding_completed = false` on contact
3. Create initial HairProfile custom object (empty, associated with contact)
4. Create initial CustomerPlan custom object (free/trial tier)
5. Send welcome email with portal login link
6. Internal notification to admin about new registration

## Workflow: Profile Completed
**Trigger:** Hair Profile form submitted (from #43)

**Actions:**
1. Set `onboarding_completed = true` on contact
2. Send "Profile Complete" confirmation email

## Steps
- [ ] Create registration workflow
- [ ] Create profile completion workflow
- [ ] Test end-to-end: register → login → complete profile → full access
- [ ] Verify custom object records created correctly

## Depends On
- #49 access group setup
- #43 hair profile form

milestone.title: Week 1: Setup + Schemas + Memberships
milestone.description: Phase 0 (environment setup) + Phase 1 (custom object schemas, HubDB tables) + Phase 6 (membership access groups)

title: [P6] Set up membership registration workflow

body:
## Task
Set up HubSpot Memberships to restrict portal pages to registered customers.

## Access Group Setup
1. Go to Settings > Website > Private Content
2. Enable private content on the portal subdomain
3. Create access group: **"Portal Customers"**
4. Add contacts to this group (can be via list or manual)

## Gate Pages
Apply the "Portal Customers" access group to ALL portal pages:
- `/portal/` (dashboard)
- `/portal/orders`
- `/portal/orders/{slug}` (order detail)
- `/portal/profile`
- `/portal/customization`
- `/portal/shop`
- `/portal/billing`
- `/portal/invoices`
- `/portal/locations`
- `/portal/settings`
- `/portal/support`
- `/portal/help`

## System Templates
Assign custom system templates (from #42):
- Login page → `membership-login.html`
- Registration page → `membership-register.html`
- Password reset → `password-reset.html`

## Steps
- [ ] Enable private content on subdomain
- [ ] Create "Portal Customers" access group
- [ ] Create contact list for auto-adding to access group
- [ ] Create workflow: new customer signup → add to Portal Customers group
- [ ] Gate all portal pages with access group
- [ ] Assign custom system templates
- [ ] Test login → redirect → gated page access
- [ ] Test non-member access → redirects to login

## Depends On
- #42 system templates
- #5 custom subdomain setup

milestone.title: Week 1: Setup + Schemas + Memberships
milestone.description: Phase 0 (environment setup) + Phase 1 (custom object schemas, HubDB tables) + Phase 6 (membership access groups)

title: [P6] Create membership access group + gate all portal pages

body:
## Task
Create a form for managing notification preferences.

## Form Fields (mapped to Contact properties):
- `email_notifications` — Checkbox/toggle
- `sms_notifications` — Checkbox/toggle
- `marketing_opt_in` — Checkbox/toggle
- `order_updates_email` — Checkbox/toggle
- `promotional_emails` — Checkbox/toggle

## Form Settings
- Pre-populate with current preferences
- On submit: update Contact properties
- Stay on page (no redirect) or show success message
- Must comply with CAN-SPAM / GDPR for marketing opt-in

## Steps
- [ ] Create form in HubSpot
- [ ] Map to contact boolean properties
- [ ] Enable pre-population
- [ ] Embed in settings-notifications.module (#39)
- [ ] Test toggle persistence

body:
## Task
Create a form for subscription plan change requests.

## Form Fields:
- `current_plan` — Hidden field (auto-populated)
- `requested_plan` — Dropdown (plans from HubDB)
- `reason` — Textarea (optional)
- `effective_date` — Date picker (optional)

## Form Settings
- On submit: create internal notification/task for admin
- Send confirmation email to customer
- Redirect to `/portal/billing`

**Note:** Actual plan changes happen manually or via integration — this form is a request.

## Steps
- [ ] Create form in HubSpot
- [ ] Create workflow: on submit → notify admin + confirm to customer
- [ ] Set redirect URL
- [ ] Embed in billing page (#41)
- [ ] Test full flow

body:
## Task
Create a support ticket submission form.

## Form Fields:
- `subject` — Text (required)
- `category` — Dropdown (general, order_issue, billing, technical, product_quality, scheduling)
- `priority` — Dropdown (low, medium, high, urgent)
- `description` — Textarea (required)
- `attachment` — File upload (optional)
- `related_order_number` — Text (optional, link to existing order)

## Form Settings
- On submit: create HubSpot Ticket (native object)
- Associate ticket with contact automatically
- Set ticket pipeline + stage
- Send confirmation email to customer via workflow
- Redirect to `/portal/support`

## Steps
- [ ] Create form in HubSpot
- [ ] Map to native Ticket object properties
- [ ] Create workflow: on submit → create ticket + email confirmation
- [ ] Set ticket pipeline (Customer Support)
- [ ] Embed in support page
- [ ] Test full flow: submit → ticket in Service Hub

body:
## Task
Create a form for customers to save new customization templates.

## Form Fields (mapped to CustomizationTemplate custom object):
- `template_name` — Text (required)
- `style` — Dropdown
- `density` — Dropdown (light, medium, heavy)
- `color` — Text
- `color_blend_percentage` — Number (0-100)
- `base_type` — Dropdown (lace, poly, mono, silk)
- `base_size` — Text
- `attachment_method` — Dropdown (tape, clips, glue, weave)
- `notes` — Textarea
- `is_default` — Checkbox (boolean)

## Form Settings
- On submit: create new CustomizationTemplate record
- Associate with contact via workflow
- Redirect to `/portal/customization`
- Show success notification

## Steps
- [ ] Create form in HubSpot
- [ ] Map fields to CustomizationTemplate custom object properties
- [ ] Create workflow: on submit → create CustomizationTemplate + associate to contact
- [ ] Set redirect URL
- [ ] Embed in customization page (#36)
- [ ] Test: submit → new template appears in customization grid

body:
## Task
Create the Hair Profile intake/update form in HubSpot.

## Form Fields (mapped to HairProfile custom object):
**Measurements:**
- `head_circumference` — Number
- `front_to_nape` — Number
- `ear_to_ear` — Number
- `temple_to_temple` — Number

**Preferences:**
- `preferred_style` — Dropdown (list from current schema)
- `preferred_density` — Dropdown (light, medium, heavy)
- `preferred_color` — Text
- `preferred_base_type` — Dropdown (lace, poly, mono, silk)
- `preferred_attachment` — Dropdown (tape, clips, glue, weave)

**Lifestyle:**
- `activity_level` — Dropdown (sedentary, moderate, active, very_active)
- `sweating_level` — Dropdown (none, light, moderate, heavy)
- `work_environment` — Text
- `sports_activities` — Text

**Photos:**
- `photo_front_url` — File upload
- `photo_side_url` — File upload
- `photo_back_url` — File upload
- `photo_top_url` — File upload

**Additional:**
- `notes` — Textarea
- `allergies` — Textarea

## Form Settings
- On submit: update existing HairProfile record (or create new one)
- Associate with contact via workflow
- Redirect back to `/portal/profile` on submit
- Set `onboarding_completed = true` on contact via workflow

## Steps
- [ ] Create form in HubSpot (Marketing > Forms)
- [ ] Map fields to HairProfile custom object properties
- [ ] Create workflow: on form submit → create/update HairProfile + associate to contact
- [ ] Set redirect URL
- [ ] Embed in hair-profile-form.module (#35)
- [ ] Test full flow: submit → data appears in CRM

body:
## Task
Build the membership system templates required by HubSpot Memberships.

## Templates:

### `src/templates/system/membership-login.html`
- Login form page (HubSpot renders the login form automatically)
- Branding: logo, brand colors, "Welcome back" messaging
- Link to register and forgot password pages

### `src/templates/system/membership-register.html`
- Registration form page
- Branding consistent with login
- Terms of service link

### `src/templates/system/password-reset.html`
- Password reset request page
- "Check your email" confirmation state

### `src/templates/system/error-404.html`
- Custom 404 page with portal branding
- Link back to dashboard

### `src/templates/system/error-500.html`
- Custom 500 page
- "Something went wrong" messaging

## Steps
- [ ] Create membership-login.html
- [ ] Create membership-register.html
- [ ] Create password-reset.html
- [ ] Create error-404.html
- [ ] Create error-500.html
- [ ] Style all system templates with portal brand

## Notes
- HubSpot injects the actual forms — we just provide the page wrapper and styling
- These are assigned in Content > Website Pages > System Pages

body:
## Task
Build the billing page showing current plan and available plans.

## Template: `src/templates/portal-billing.html`
- `dataQueryPath: ../data-queries/customer_billing`

## Modules:

### `billing-current.module`
- Shows current plan: plan name, tier, price, billing cycle, next billing date
- Payment method info (last 4 digits if stored)
- Plan status badge (active, trialing, past_due)

### `billing-plans.module`
- Grid of available plans from HubDB `subscription_plans` table
- Each card: plan name, price, features list, "Current" badge or "Upgrade/Downgrade" button
- Plan change button → HubSpot form (Phase 5)

**Note:** For actual payment processing, may need Stripe integration via serverless function or external link.

## Steps
- [ ] Create billing-current.module
- [ ] Create billing-plans.module
- [ ] Create portal-billing.html template
- [ ] Style plan comparison cards
- [ ] Test with active plan data

## Depends On
- #25 customer_billing.graphql
- #12 HubDB subscription_plans table (Phase 1)
- Phase 5 plan change form

body:
## Task
Build the shop page showing available products from HubDB.

## Template: `src/templates/portal-shop.html`
- `dataQueryPath: ../data-queries/products_catalog`
- OR uses `{% for row in hubdb_table_rows() %}` HubL tag directly (simpler)

## Module: `product-grid.module`
- Product card grid from HubDB `products` table
- Each card: product image, name, description, price, category badge
- Category filter tabs/buttons (e.g., Hair Systems, Accessories, Maintenance)
- Sort by price, name, newest
- "Add to Cart" or "Inquire" button → links to Shopify checkout or HubSpot form

**Note:** If using HubDB rows directly, may not need a GraphQL data query.

## Steps
- [ ] Create product-grid.module
- [ ] Add category filtering
- [ ] Add sort functionality
- [ ] Create portal-shop.html template
- [ ] Connect to HubDB products table
- [ ] Test with sample products

## Depends On
- #11 HubDB products table (Phase 1)

Build the customization page showing saved customization templates and ability to create new ones.

## Template: `src/templates/portal-customization.html`
- `dataQueryPath: ../data-queries/customer_templates`

## Module: `customization-grid.module`
- Card grid layout for saved templates
- Each card shows: template name, style, density, color, base type, attachment method, created date
- "Default" badge for `is_default = true`
- Actions per card: Set as default, Edit, Delete
- "Create New Template" button → opens form (Phase 5)

**Empty State:** No templates saved yet → CTA to create first template

## Steps
- [ ] Create customization-grid.module
- [ ] Style card layout with responsive grid
- [ ] Add template actions (set default, edit — these may need serverless functions)
- [ ] Create portal-customization.html template
- [ ] Test with multiple templates and empty state

- Phase 5 new customization template form

body:
## Task
Build the hair profile page — shows current profile data and link to the update form.

## Template: `src/templates/portal-profile.html`
- `dataQueryPath: ../data-queries/hair_profile`

## Modules:

### `hair-profile-display.module`
Read-only display of all profile data in organized sections:
- **Measurements**: head circumference, front-to-nape, ear-to-ear, temple-to-temple
- **Preferences**: style, density, color, base type, attachment method
- **Lifestyle**: activity level, sweating, work environment, sports
- **Photos**: Display photo URLs as images (front, side, back, top)

If no profile exists (onboarding_completed = false):
- Show "Complete your hair profile" CTA

### `hair-profile-form.module`
- Contains a HubSpot form embed (`{% form %}`) for creating/updating the profile
- Or links to a dedicated form page

## Steps
- [ ] Create hair-profile-display.module
- [ ] Create hair-profile-form.module (form embed)
- [ ] Create portal-profile.html template
- [ ] Style photo grid display
- [ ] Test both states: profile exists vs. no profile

## Depends On
- #23 hair_profile.graphql
- #39 Hair Profile form (Phase 5)

milestone.title: Week 3: Core Pages — Dashboard, Orders, Profile
milestone.description: Phase 4 part 1: Build templates + modules for dashboard, orders list, order detail, hair profile

title: [P4] Build hair profile page: template + modules

body:
## Task
Build the order detail dynamic page showing full order info + production timeline.

## Template: `src/templates/portal-order-detail.html`
- Dynamic page template — uses `request.path_param_dict.dynamic_slug` for order_number
- `dataQueryPath: ../data-queries/order_detail`

## Module: `order-detail.module`
- `data_query_path` in meta.json points to order_detail.graphql

**Displays:**
- Order header: order number, product name, status badge, dates
- Order totals: subtotal, tax, shipping, total
- Production timeline/progress bar: visual stages from awaiting_confirmation → completed
- Status history: chronological list of status changes with dates and notes
- Configuration snapshot: display the saved customization details
- Customer notes

**Security:** Check that `contact_collection` association contains the logged-in user. If empty → show "Order not found."

## Steps
- [ ] Create order-detail.module
- [ ] Create production timeline component (visual progress bar)
- [ ] Create portal-order-detail.html template
- [ ] Test with dynamic URL: `/portal/orders/ORD-001`
- [ ] Verify contact ownership check

## Depends On
- #22 order_detail.graphql

Build the orders listing page showing all customer orders with status.

## Module: `order-list.module`
**Displays:**
- Full table of orders: order number, product name, status badge, production stage, total, date
- Color-coded status badges (pending=yellow, confirmed=blue, in_production=purple, shipped=orange, delivered=green, cancelled=red)
- Each row links to order detail page: `/portal/orders/ORDER_NUMBER`
- "New Order" CTA button

## Steps

body:
## Task
Build the dashboard page — the portal home that shows overview stats, recent orders, and quick actions.

## Template: `src/templates/portal-dashboard.html`
- Extends `portal.html` layout
- `dataQueryPath: ../data-queries/dashboard`
- Contains: dashboard-stats, production-alert, recent-orders, quick-actions modules

## Modules to build:

### `dashboard-stats.module`
Displays 3-4 stat cards:
- Total orders count (`associations.p_order_collection__primary.total`)
- Active orders count (filter by status != delivered/cancelled)
- Hair profile completion (onboarding_completed boolean)
- Current plan name

### `production-alert.module`
Shows next estimated completion date from most recent in-production order.

### `recent-orders.module`
Table showing last 5 orders: order number, product, status badge, total, date.
Links each row to `/portal/orders?order=ORDER_NUMBER`

### `quick-actions.module`
Grid of action buttons: New Order, View Profile, Browse Shop, Get Help

## Data Access Pattern
```
{% set contact = data_query.data.CRM.contact %}
{% set orders = contact.associations.p_order_collection__primary.items %}
{% set profile = contact.associations.p_hair_profile_collection__primary.items|first %}
```

## Steps
- [ ] Create dashboard-stats.module
- [ ] Create production-alert.module
- [ ] Create recent-orders.module
- [ ] Create quick-actions.module
- [ ] Create portal-dashboard.html template
- [ ] Upload and test with sample data
- [ ] Verify data binds correctly for a logged-in contact

## Depends On
- #18 Portal layout
- #20 dashboard.graphql

body:
## Task
Build the global sidebar navigation module used on every portal page.

## Module: `src/modules/portal-sidebar.module/`

**Files:**
- `meta.json` — global: true, host_template_types: ["PAGE"]
- `fields.json` — logo image, nav items (configurable)
- `module.html` — Sidebar with logo, nav links, active state detection
- `module.css` — Sidebar styles (fixed left, responsive collapse)

**Navigation Items:**
- Dashboard → `/portal`
- Orders → `/portal/orders`
- Hair Profile → `/portal/profile`
- Customization → `/portal/customization`
- Shop → `/portal/shop`
- Billing → `/portal/billing`
- Invoices → `/portal/invoices`
- Locations → `/portal/locations`
- Support → `/portal/support` (link to Service Hub customer portal)
- Help → `/portal/help` (link to Knowledge Base)
- Settings → `/portal/settings`

**Active state:** Use `{{ request.path }}` to highlight current page in nav.

## Steps
- [ ] Create module directory with all 4 files
- [ ] Implement responsive sidebar (hamburger menu on mobile)
- [ ] Test active state highlighting
- [ ] Upload and verify in Design Manager

body:
## Task
Fetch product catalog from HubDB for the shop page.

## File: `src/data-queries/products.graphql`

```graphql
# label: "Portal Shop"
# description: "Product catalog from HubDB"
query products {
  HUBDB {
    products_collection(filter: { in_stock__eq: true }, orderBy: "display_order") {
      items {
        name
        slug
        short_description
        price
        compare_at_price
        currency
        category
        primary_image
        featured
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Note: No contact scoping needed — all logged-in customers see the same product catalog.

## Steps
- [ ] Create .graphql file
- [ ] Test in GraphiQL

## Depends On
- #14 HubDB products table

body:
## Task
Build the CSS foundation for the portal theme.

## Files to create:

### `src/css/main.css`
- CSS reset / normalize
- CSS custom properties (colors, fonts, spacing, radii)
- Base typography
- Utility classes

### `src/css/portal-layout.css`
- Sidebar layout (fixed left, collapsible on mobile)
- Portal header bar
- Content area with proper padding
- Responsive breakpoints (sidebar → hamburger on mobile)

### `src/css/components/cards.css`
- Card base styles, card grid layouts

### `src/css/components/tables.css`
- Data table styles (order list, invoice list)

### `src/css/components/badges.css`
- Status badges (color-coded: pending=yellow, active=green, etc.)

### `src/css/components/forms.css`
- Override HubSpot form defaults with portal branding

### `src/css/components/buttons.css`
- Primary, secondary, outline, danger button styles

### `src/css/templates/system.css`
- Login/register/reset page styling

## Design Reference
Match the existing Next.js portal look and feel as closely as possible using CSS.

## Steps
- [ ] Create main.css with CSS variables matching current Tailwind theme
- [ ] Create portal-layout.css with sidebar/header/content layout
- [ ] Create all component CSS files
- [ ] Create system.css for auth pages
- [ ] Test by uploading and previewing in HubSpot

body:
## Task Create the portal-specific layout that adds the sidebar + header bar. All portal pages extend this. ## File: `src/templates/layouts/portal.html` This template: 1. Extends `base.html` 2. Overrides the `{% block body %}` section 3. Adds the portal sidebar (global module) 4. Adds the portal header bar showing logged-in user name 5. Defines `{% block portal_content %}` for page-specific content ```html {% extends "./base.html" %} {% block header %}{% endblock %} {% block footer %}{% endblock %} {% block body %} {{ require_css(get_asset_url("../../css/portal-layout.css")) }}
{% global_partial path="../partials/portal-sidebar.html" %}
{% module "portal_header" path="../../modules/portal-header.module" %}
{% block portal_content %}{% endblock %}
{% endblock body %} ``` ## Steps - [ ] Create `src/templates/layouts/portal.html` - [ ] Create `src/templates/partials/portal-sidebar.html` (sidebar nav partial) - [ ] Ensure portal layout hides the standard header/footer - [ ] Test with a blank portal page

body:
## Task
Create the theme configuration files.

## theme.json
```json
{
  "label": "Hair Solutions Customer Portal",
  "preview_path": "./templates/portal-dashboard.html",
  "screenshot_path": "./images/template-previews/dashboard.png",
  "enable_domain_stylesheets": false,
  "responsive_breakpoints": [
    {
      "name": "mobile",
      "mediaQuery": "@media (max-width: 767px)",
      "previewWidth": { "value": 477 }
    }
  ]
}
```

## fields.json
Define global theme fields:
- [ ] Brand colors (primary, secondary, accent, success, warning, error)
- [ ] Font families (heading, body)
- [ ] Portal sidebar width
- [ ] Logo image

## Steps
- [ ] Create `src/theme.json`
- [ ] Create `src/fields.json` with brand variables
- [ ] Test upload: `hs upload src hair-solutions-portal`

body:
## Task
Create the full theme directory structure locally. This is the foundation for all CMS work.

## Directory Structure
```
hair-solutions-portal/
├── schemas/
├── hubdb/
└── src/
    ├── theme.json
    ├── fields.json
    ├── css/
    │   ├── main.css
    │   ├── portal-layout.css
    │   ├── components/
    │   │   ├── cards.css
    │   │   ├── tables.css
    │   │   ├── badges.css
    │   │   ├── forms.css
    │   │   └── buttons.css
    │   └── templates/
    │       └── system.css
    ├── js/
    │   └── main.js
    ├── images/
    │   ├── logo.svg
    │   ├── module-icons/
    │   └── template-previews/
    ├── data-queries/
    ├── modules/
    ├── templates/
    │   ├── layouts/
    │   ├── partials/
    │   └── system/
    └── sections/
```

## Steps
- [ ] Create all directories
- [ ] Create placeholder files where needed
- [ ] Verify structure mirrors [recruiting-agency-graphql-theme](https://github.com/HubSpot/recruiting-agency-graphql-theme) pattern

## Reference
Based on `HubSpot/recruiting-agency-graphql-theme` + `HubSpot/cms-theme-boilerplate`

| `featured` | boolean | |

## Task
Create a HubDB table for the subscription plan catalog (displayed on billing page).

## Table: `subscription_plans`

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `name` | text | Plan name (e.g., "Monthly Essential") |
| `description` | rich text | Plan description |
| `price` | number | Monthly/yearly price |
| `currency` | text | Default: USD |
| `interval` | select | month, year |
| `systems_per_year` | number | |
| `features` | rich text | Comma-separated or HTML list |
| `is_active` | boolean | |
| `display_order` | number | Sort order |

## Steps
- [ ] Create table in HubSpot (Settings → HubDB) or via API
- [ ] Add seed data rows for your plan tiers
- [ ] Publish the table
- [ ] Test via GraphiQL: `HUBDB { subscription_plans_collection { items { name price } } }`

## Seed Data
Create rows for each plan tier with accurate pricing and features.

## Task
Add custom property groups to the native Contact object for subscription and notification data.

## Group: "Subscription Plan"
| Property | Type | Field Type |
|---|---|---|
| `current_plan` | string | text |
| `plan_price` | number | number |
| `billing_interval` | enumeration | select | month, year |
| `period_start` | date | date |
| `period_end` | date | date |
| `plan_status` | enumeration | select | active, cancelled, past_due |

## Group: "Notification Preferences"
| Property | Type | Field Type |
|---|---|---|
| `notify_order_updates` | enumeration | booleancheckbox |
| `notify_production_reminders` | enumeration | booleancheckbox |
| `notify_marketing` | enumeration | booleancheckbox |

## Group: "Portal"
| Property | Type | Field Type |
|---|---|---|
| `is_portal_customer` | enumeration | booleancheckbox | Used for membership access group |

## Steps
- [ ] Create property group "Subscription Plan" via API (`POST /crm/v3/properties/contacts`)
- [ ] Create property group "Notification Preferences"
- [ ] Create property group "Portal"
- [ ] Add all properties to their groups
- [ ] Verify properties visible on Contact records in HubSpot

body:
## Task
Create the Invoice custom object.

## Schema: `schemas/invoice.json`

**Properties:**
| Property | Type | Field Type |
|---|---|---|
| `invoice_number` | string | text | `hasUniqueValue: true` |
| `amount` | number | number |
| `tax` | number | number |
| `total` | number | number |
| `currency` | string | text |
| `status` | enumeration | select | pending, paid, overdue, cancelled |
| `issue_date` | date | date |
| `due_date` | date | date |
| `paid_at` | date | date |
| `pdf_url` | string | text | Link to PDF file |
| `description` | string | textarea |

**Config:**
- `primaryDisplayProperty`: `invoice_number`
- `associatedObjects`: `["CONTACT", "2-XXXXX"]` (Contact + Order)
- `requiredProperties`: `["invoice_number", "total"]`

## Steps
- [ ] Create `schemas/invoice.json`
- [ ] POST schema via API (after Order object exists for association)
- [ ] Verify associations to Contact and Order

- `associatedObjects`: `["CONTACT"]`

body:
## Task
Create the Order custom object via API.

## Schema: `schemas/order.json`

**Properties:**
| Property | Type | Field Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `order_number` | string | text | `hasUniqueValue: true` — used as dynamic page slug |
| `product_name` | string | text | |
| `status` | enumeration | select | pending, confirmed, in_production, quality_check, shipped, delivered, cancelled |
| `production_stage` | enumeration | select | awaiting_confirmation, materials_preparation, base_construction, hair_ventilation, styling, quality_inspection, packaging, completed |
| `subtotal` | number | number | |
| `tax` | number | number | |
| `shipping` | number | number | |
| `total` | number | number | |
| `currency` | string | text | Default: USD |
| `estimated_production_start` | date | date | |
| `estimated_completion` | date | date | |
| `actual_completion_date` | date | date | |
| `shipped_at` | date | date | |
| `delivered_at` | date | date | |
| `customer_notes` | string | textarea | |
| `configuration_snapshot` | string | textarea | JSON string of config at time of order |

**Config:**
- `primaryDisplayProperty`: `order_number`
- `associatedObjects`: `["CONTACT"]`
- `requiredProperties`: `["order_number", "product_name"]`
- `searchableProperties`: `["order_number", "product_name"]`

## Steps
- [ ] Create `schemas/order.json`
- [ ] POST schema via API
- [ ] Verify in HubSpot CRM
- [ ] Confirm `order_number` has unique value constraint

milestone.title: Week 1: Setup + Schemas + Memberships
milestone.description: Phase 0 (environment setup) + Phase 1 (custom object schemas, HubDB tables) + Phase 6 (membership access groups)

title: [P1] Create Order custom object schema

body:
## Task
Create the Hair Profile custom object via API (`POST /crm/v3/schemas`).

## Schema: `schemas/hair_profile.json`

**Properties:**
| Property | Type | Field Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `head_circumference` | number | number | In cm/inches |
| `front_to_nape` | number | number | |
| `ear_to_ear` | number | number | |
| `temple_to_temple` | number | number | |
| `preferred_style` | string | text | |
| `density` | enumeration | select | light, medium, heavy |
| `hair_color` | string | text | |
| `base_type` | enumeration | select | lace, skin, mono |
| `attachment_method` | enumeration | select | tape, glue, clips |
| `activity_level` | string | text | |
| `sweating_level` | enumeration | select | low, moderate, high |
| `work_environment` | string | text | |
| `sports_activities` | string | textarea | |
| `photo_front` | string | file | |
| `photo_side` | string | file | |
| `photo_back` | string | file | |
| `photo_top` | string | file | |
| `notes` | string | textarea | |
| `onboarding_completed` | enumeration | booleancheckbox | |

**Config:**
- `primaryDisplayProperty`: `preferred_style`
- `associatedObjects`: `["CONTACT"]`
- `searchableProperties`: `["hair_color", "base_type"]`

## Steps
- [ ] Create `schemas/hair_profile.json` in repo
- [ ] POST schema via API or `hs` CLI
- [ ] Verify object appears in HubSpot CRM settings
- [ ] Verify association to Contacts works

## Reference
- [Custom Objects API](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/guides/api/crm/objects/custom-objects)
- Pattern: `schemas/` dir in [recruiting-agency-graphql-theme](https://github.com/HubSpot/recruiting-agency-graphql-theme/tree/main/schemas)

title: [P1] Create Hair Profile custom object schema

body:
## Task
Connect a custom subdomain for the customer portal. Required for membership functionality.

## Steps
- [ ] Choose subdomain (e.g., `portal.hairsolutions.co`)
- [ ] In HubSpot: Settings → Domains & URLs → Connect a domain
- [ ] Add DNS CNAME record pointing to HubSpot
- [ ] Verify domain connection
- [ ] Set as primary domain for website pages

## Important
- Memberships do NOT work on default `hs-sites.com` domains
- SSL is automatically provisioned by HubSpot

## Acceptance Criteria
- Custom subdomain resolves to HubSpot
- SSL certificate active

milestone.title: Week 1: Setup + Schemas + Memberships
milestone.description: Phase 0 (environment setup) + Phase 1 (custom object schemas, HubDB tables) + Phase 6 (membership access groups)

title: [P0] Connect custom subdomain for portal

body:
## Task
Create a HubSpot sandbox account for safe development and testing.

## Steps
- [ ] Run `hs sandbox create` to create a standard sandbox
- [ ] Name it something identifiable (e.g., `hair-solutions-dev`)
- [ ] Set as default: `hs accounts use hair-solutions-dev`
- [ ] Verify sandbox is active in HubSpot UI

## Acceptance Criteria
- Sandbox account accessible via CLI and HubSpot UI
- All development/testing happens in sandbox first

milestone.title: Week 1: Setup + Schemas + Memberships

title: [P0] Create development sandbox account

body:
## Task
Install and configure the HubSpot CLI for local development.

## Steps
- [ ] Install HubSpot CLI: `npm install -g @hubspot/cli`
- [ ] Run `hs init` and paste your Personal Access Key (PAK)
- [ ] Verify connection: `hs accounts list`
- [ ] Confirm `hubspot.config.yml` is created
- [ ] Add `hubspot.config.yml` to `.gitignore`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `hs accounts list` shows connected account
- Config file is gitignored

## References
- [HubSpot CLI repo](https://github.com/HubSpot/hubspot-cli)
- PAK scopes needed: `content`, `design_manager`, `hubdb`, `collector.graphql_schema.read`, `collector.graphql_query.execute`, `crm.objects.*`, `crm.schemas.*`, `developer.projects.write`
